title: chenopodium × pelgrimsianum
intro: Chenopodium × pelgrimsianum is a sterile hybrid plant species within the Chenopodium genus, resulting from the cross between Chenopodium album and Chenopodium ambinum, and is commonly known as the "Fat Hen" or "Fatweed". This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its high content of antioxidants and vitamins. The health benefits of Chenopodium × pelgrimsianum include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used in cooking as a nutritious and edible green.
